commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-030 slice B — apply_close_out.py extensions for five new close-out payload sections
commit_message_full:
v2: PI-030 slice B — apply_close_out.py extensions for five new close-out payload sections

apply_close_out.py now lands all five new sections introduced by the
Code Change Lifecycle methodology + DEC-223:

  session → conversation → work_tickets → planning_items → commits
         → decisions → references → resolves_planning_items
         → addresses_planning_items

Section shape transforms:
  - work_tickets[].addresses_planning_item → embedded addresses ref
    (atomic with work_ticket POST; requires work_ticket_identifier in
    the entry so the embedded ref's source_id is explicit)
  - commits[].commit_conversation_id auto-populated from the payload's
    conversation block
  - resolves_planning_items[] → POST /references with relationship=resolves
    (slice A's atomic edge+flip fires server-side)
  - addresses_planning_items[] → POST /references with relationship=addresses

Architecture changes in apply_close_out.py:
  - _SECTION_ENDPOINTS replaced with _SECTIONS (NamedTuple-based; NamedTuple
    chosen over @dataclass to avoid a Python 3.12 dataclass introspection
    quirk under the test-loader pattern spec_from_file_location)
  - Per-section optional shape_fn(entry, context) -> body translation
  - Context dict threaded through the loop, populated from the payload's
    conversation block; shape functions read conversation_identifier
  - _record_target_id extended for the v0.8 entity types' prefixed
    identifier fields (conversation_identifier, work_ticket_identifier,
    commit_identifier)
  - _record_label extended for the prefixed identifiers and the
    resolves/addresses entries' planning_item_identifier
  - ValueError from a shape function fails the apply with kind=shape_error
    captured in deposit_event_error_info; subsequent sections still attempt

vocab.py extended to admit conversation, work_ticket, and commit as
deposit_event_wrote_record target_types. The access-layer invariant
sum(records_summary) == len(wrote_records) holds for the new types
without skipping (the references skip pattern is preserved as the
DEC-215 Option I documented exception).

conversations.py: surgical fix — when an explicit identifier is provided
and matches an existing row, raise ConflictError BEFORE the duplicate-
title check fires. Previously the title-duplicate check ran first,
which surfaced a 422 "duplicate title" on a re-applied close-out
payload (because the conversation_title was the same) instead of the
correct 409 "already exists". The fix restores apply idempotency on
re-run for payloads that include a conversation block.

Backward compatibility: payloads without the new sections (v0.7 format)
apply unchanged. Sections absent or empty are skipped (no-op). HTTP 409
SKIP idempotency preserved across all sections including conversation.

Tests: 12 new in test_apply_close_out.py (TestPI030NewSections) covering
conversation block creation with the records_session edge, commits FK
propagation, missing-conversation-block shape error, work_ticket embedded
addresses ref, resolves/addresses translation, section ordering per
methodology §4, full-payload idempotency on re-run, v0.7 backward
compatibility, the v0.8 audit chain via wrote_record edges, atomic
conversation+refs rollback on conversation POST failure, and resolves
idempotency on an already-Resolved planning_item. Plus 3 new in
test_vocab_v0_8.py for the new wrote_record target_types. Baseline 1486
+ 15 = 1501 passed.

Authority: DEC-221 (full scope), DEC-222 (emit-time helper, full
records in payload), DEC-223 (conversation block), DEC-224 (resolves
extension).

commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-030 slice C — enumerate_commits.py helper for close-out commit ingestion
commit_message_full:
v2: PI-030 slice C — enumerate_commits.py helper for close-out commit ingestion

New standalone script that produces the JSON array suitable to paste
into a close-out payload's commits section. Sandbox-side use, invoked
at close-out authoring time per methodology §5.5.

Mechanics:
- Reads db-export/commits.json to find the per-repo last-ingested SHA
  (highest commit_committed_at value for that repository).
- Runs git pull --ff-only per repo (skippable via --skip-pull for tests).
- Enumerates commits via 'git log --reverse <since>..HEAD --format=...'
  using ASCII control-character separators (US/RS) for safe multi-line
  commit message parsing. --reverse asks git for chronological order
  directly, matching CM-NNNN assignment order at apply time.
- Counts files-changed per SHA via git diff-tree --root (the --root
  flag ensures the initial commit compares against the empty tree
  rather than emitting nothing).
- Emits JSON in commit.md §3.2 shape.

CLI surface:
- --repos crmbuilder[,ClevelandBusinessMentoring]
- --engagement-db-export <path>
- --repo-root <path> + --repo-root-override REPO=PATH for multi-repo
- --branch main
- --skip-pull
- --output <path> (default stdout)

Tests: 8 covering happy path, bootstrap (no snapshot), since-snapshot
filtering, multi-line message parsing, merge commit parent extraction,
lowercase SHA normalization, non-git repo error, CLI end-to-end.

Authority: DEC-222 (emit-time helper, full records in payload),
methodology §5.5 (commit enumeration range).

commit_message_full:
v2: Path B shelved — Cloudflare Managed OAuth configured but blocked by upstream claude.ai connector bug

This commits the local Path B implementation work toward PI-049 / DEC-226 (replace X-CRMBuilder-Secret middleware with OAuth 2.1 + PKCE auth via Cloudflare Access). Path B was configured end-to-end: middleware.py and the X-CRMBuilder-Secret enforcement removed; server.py wires streamable_http_path='/' so user-entered URL matches the PRM resource field exactly (Anthropic connector spec requirement); config.py drops the mcp_shared_secret setting; Cloudflare-side: existing self-hosted CRM Builder MCP Access app has Managed OAuth enabled with claude.ai/claude.com redirect URIs registered, RFC 8707 compliant, PKCE S256, DCR endpoint live; end-to-end OAuth dance succeeds through the consent step. Outcome: claude.ai connector registration FAILS post-consent with the ofid_* reference-ID error pattern documented in Anthropic GitHub issue #271 and 6+ sibling issues across WorkOS, Clerk, Salesforce, M365, and now Cloudflare. The MCP HTTP transport is left in its current OAuth-ready state, preserved as-is so that if Anthropic eventually fixes the connector bug, claude.ai-web integration is immediately available with no further code work. MCP stdio mode for Claude Desktop continues to work unchanged.

commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-052 Slice A — terminal proof-of-concept for Anthropic-API chat loop with three read-only governance tools
commit_message_full:
v2: PI-052 Slice A — terminal proof-of-concept for Anthropic-API chat loop with three read-only governance tools

WT-048. Per DEC-261 (slicing plan) and DEC-257 (streaming on for the
production UI — explicitly deferred for this spike), this is the
foundational architecture validation: a ~130 LOC Python script that runs
a blocking chat loop with three hand-defined tools (get_current_status,
get_current_charter, list_recent_sessions), validating that the
Anthropic SDK + native tools=[...] + httpx against the local REST API
compose into a working chat loop before any PySide6 code lands.

No PySide6, no streaming, no caching, no persistence. Slice B is the
first slice with production code. The script is throwaway —
committed as a reference but not maintained.

Implementation notes:
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env required; prints to stderr and exits 1 if missing
  (no keyring path per DEC-254 — Slice B adds that).
- Three tools defined inline as JSON Schema in the TOOLS list. No reuse
  of mcp_server.tools (Slice C does the refactor per design Appendix A).
- httpx.Client (blocking) against http://127.0.0.1:8765 with 30s timeout.
- Multi-tool turn handled in the inner while loop — collects every
  tool_use block in one assistant message, dispatches each, appends a
  single tool_result message with the full batch.
- Tool call echo to stderr (one line per call: → name(args)).
- Final assistant text printed to stdout once stop_reason != tool_use.
- Ctrl+C and EOF both exit cleanly with code 0.
- Tool errors caught and returned as is_error tool_result blocks so the
  assistant sees them rather than the script crashing.

Smoke-tested the three dispatch paths against the live REST API
(returned status singleton, charter singleton, recent sessions list
with default-3 and explicit limit=2). The live Anthropic chat loop
test (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY required) is Doug's manual gate per the
kickoff acceptance criteria.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit_message_full:
Expand glossary to v0.2: add Skill, Pattern, Inventory, Client (TERM-002 through TERM-005)

Four glossary entries added per session discussion:

- Skill (TERM-002): reusable domain-specific knowledge file, loaded contextually on client defining statements
- Pattern (TERM-003): reusable structural template for a business domain or org type
- Inventory (TERM-004): reference list of typical items for a domain or org type

commit_message_first_line: v2: align governance-recording-rules with DEC-299 conceptual model + add Mandatory Logging and stop-and-log discipline
commit_message_full:
v2: align governance-recording-rules with DEC-299 conceptual model + add Mandatory Logging and stop-and-log discipline

Substantial conceptual rewrite triggered by Doug's observation that the
document was silent on (a) the requirement to log every Claude.ai chat
operating against V2, and (b) the discipline at conversation boundaries
within a multi-conversation session.

The investigation surfaced two prior errors in v0.1:

- I had framed Session as the close-out completion record and
  Conversation as one-per-Claude.ai-chat. DEC-299 (already Active in
  V2) inverts this: Session is the medium-agnostic communication unit
  (1 Claude.ai chat = 1 session); Conversation is a topical sub-unit
  (1:N within session). DEC-299 directs that documentation use the new
  conceptual model even before PI-073's schema migration completes.

- I had documented the close-out payload as a 'four-section shape'
  drawn from SES-046 through SES-052 precedent, when the actual current
  schema is v0.8 with ten elements (label + nine record sections), as
  documented in CLAUDE.md line 106.

Changes in this commit:

- §0: added Mandatory Logging as a second core principle alongside
  API/MCP-only. Every session against a V2 engagement is logged. No
  off-the-record communication.
- §3: rewrote Session framing to medium-agnostic communication unit
  per DEC-299. Added medium-classifier note pointing at PI-073 open
  design questions.
- §4: rewrote Conversation framing to topical sub-unit per DEC-299.
  Added stop-and-log discipline with two escape hatches: close current
  conversation and open new one in same session, OR suggest new
  session. Added boundary heuristics. Added authoring mechanism:
  direct API POST at boundary, not bundled in close-out.
- §9: rewrote to v0.8 ten-element payload schema (label + 9 record
  sections), correcting the prior four-section error. Added
  transitional note for singular conversation -> plural conversations
  pluralization via PI-073.
- §10: added three failure modes — topic shift without stop-and-log,
  multiple conversations conflated under v0.8 singular constraint, and
  off-the-record session.
- Revision history row added; document grew from ~2700 to 4022 words.
  Over the original v0.1 budget; trim pass deferred pending review.

commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-074 — add executive_summary column to planning_items, decisions, sessions
commit_message_full:
v2: PI-074 — add executive_summary column to planning_items, decisions, sessions

Adds a nullable ``executive_summary`` TEXT column to each of the three
governance record types so a product manager or executive reading the
governance log can see the gist of every record without parsing
implementer-facing detail. A CHECK constraint enforces 200-800
character length when the value is set; PI-075 will backfill existing
rows and tighten the column to NOT NULL in a follow-on migration.

Changes:

- Alembic revision 0020 (heads on top of 0019). Three batch_alter_table
  operations, one per target table. Each adds the column and a
  per-table named CHECK constraint
  (``ck_planning_executive_summary_length``,
  ``ck_decision_executive_summary_length``,
  ``ck_session_executive_summary_length``).
- ``migrations/env.py`` now uses a dedicated migration engine with
  ``foreign_keys=OFF`` set at connect time. SQLite batch_alter_table
  copies and drops tables; the drop trips FK enforcement on
  self-referencing rows (decisions.supersedes_id /
  superseded_by_id). Issuing PRAGMA after SQLAlchemy's connect()
  fails because the driver auto-begins a transaction. The runtime
  engine remains FK-on; only the migration engine relaxes it.
- ``access/_helpers.py`` grows ``validate_optional_length()`` — None
  and whitespace-only strings normalise to None; non-string raises
  ``ValidationError``; out-of-range strings raise with a
  field-specific message.
- Repository ``create`` and ``update`` paths on decisions,
  planning_items, and sessions accept ``executive_summary`` and
  validate via the helper. The update loop preserves explicit None
  (the column is nullable, so don't coerce to "" like the other
  legacy update fields).
- Pydantic ``Create``/``Update`` schemas grow the field on all three
  entity types. Routers pass through ``**body.model_dump()`` and
  pick up the field automatically.
- The JSON exporter is column-driven via
  ``row.__table__.columns``, so the new column flows into
  ``db-export/{planning_items,decisions,sessions}.json`` without
  exporter changes.

Validation:

- All migration, access, and API tests pass (1086 passed, 2 skipped).
- End-to-end smoke against the branch DB confirms: under-200 rejected,
  over-800 rejected, None accepted, valid 200-800 accepted.

Branch isolation: branched off ``main`` at 5dcda0e. Branch-isolated DB
copies live in ``crmbuilder-v2/data/branch-pi-074/`` (gitignored). The
live engagement DBs at ``crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements/*.db`` are not
touched by branch work.

Cross-PI note: PI-073 (Session/Conversation redesign) is in flight on
its own branch and renames ``sessions`` to the new schema. After PI-073
lands, the ``sessions.executive_summary`` column on PI-074 must be
preserved on the renamed table or migrated to ``session_executive_summary``
per the post-PI-073 parent-prefix convention. Whoever lands second
absorbs the rebase.

commit_message_full:
v2: PI-091/092/093 — surface executive_summary in planning_items, decisions, sessions desktop UI

Brings the audience-facing executive_summary field (added in PI-074) into
the v2 desktop app for all three governance entity types. PMs and
executives now see the same 200-800-character audience-readable summary
in the create / edit dialogs and on the master pane that has been
write/readable via API/MCP since PI-074. Optional until PI-075's
backfill tightens to NOT NULL.

Shared base infrastructure (consumed by all three PIs):

- FieldSchema grows ``min_length: int | None`` and ``max_length: int | None``
  attributes. When set on a ``widget="text"`` field, EntityCrudDialog
  attaches a live ``"N / max_length"`` counter beneath the
  ``QPlainTextEdit`` and enforces the range pre-submit.
- Counter color rules: neutral at N=0 and when ``min_length <= N <= max_length``;
  amber (``t("color.warning.default")``, #9D6517) when ``0 < N < min_length``;
  red (``_INLINE_ERROR_STYLE``, #B22222) when ``N > max_length``. Updates
  live on textChanged.
- Length gate runs after required / regex checks via a new
  ``_validate_lengths`` step. The inline error message uses exactly the
  wording ``validate_optional_length`` (PI-074) raises from the API:
  ``"must be {min_length}-{max_length} characters (got {N})"`` — so the
  client error and the API error are character-identical.
- Empty / whitespace-only values are always valid (the field is optional;
  the access-layer helper coerces empty/whitespace to None before the
  range check, and the UI mirrors that).
- Counter is wired through the existing ``_wrap_with_error`` container
  as an optional third row. Containers without length-bounded fields
  are unchanged.

PI-091 — planning_items:

- ``_planning_item_schema.py``: new ``executive_summary`` FieldSchema
  between ``description`` and ``item_type``. ``omit_when_empty_in_create=True``.
- ``panels/planning_items.py``: "Executive Summary" section in the
  detail pane above Description, mirroring the existing ``_long_text``
  treatment for the description field.

PI-092 — decisions:

- ``_decision_schema.py``: new ``executive_summary`` FieldSchema between
  ``status`` and ``context`` so the audience-facing summary appears
  above the implementer-facing long-form fields.
- ``panels/decisions.py``: ``("executive_summary", "Executive Summary")``
  inserted as the first entry of ``_LONG_TEXT_FIELDS``, rendering above
  Context / Decision / Rationale / Alternatives / Consequences in the
  detail pane's existing long-text loop.

PI-093 — sessions:

- ``_session_schema.py``: new ``session_executive_summary`` FieldSchema
  (using the post-PI-073 ``session_``-prefixed column name verified
  against access/models.py) between ``session_description`` and
  ``session_medium``. PI-093's "no edit dialog per DEC-013" note in its
  body is stale: DEC-314 superseded DEC-013 and SessionEditDialog
  already exists in session_create.py, so both Create and Edit pick the
  field up automatically through the shared schema list.
- ``panels/sessions.py``: "Executive Summary" form row immediately above
  the Description row in the main detail form. Empty values render as a
  dim ``—`` placeholder via ``read_only_line`` matching the panel's
  existing form-row convention.

Tests:

- ``tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_crud_dialog_base.py`` grows 11 new tests
  covering counter construction, color thresholds (neutral / amber / red),
  single-bound rendering, the length-validation gate (below min, above
  max, in range, empty optional), error-clearing on edit, and the
  no-counter-when-no-bounds default. 36 passed / 0 failed on the file.
- Targeted runs: 207 passed across planning_item / decision / crud_dialog
  scope. 6 pre-existing failures in test_orientation, test_navigation,
  test_apply_close_out, and mcp_server/test_smoke all reproduce against
  clean main (verified via stash + re-run); they sit in PI-073
  Session/Conversation redesign territory (apply_close_out hasn't yet
  caught up to the new session/conversation Pydantic shape) and are not
  regressions from this work.
- ``tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_session_create_dialog.py`` has a
  collection-time ``ImportError`` for ``SESSION_CONVERSATION_REF_PLACEHOLDER``;
  also pre-existing on clean main.

PI-091 and PI-092 are unchanged in the access layer / API since PI-074
already wired the field through. PI-093 likewise consumes
``session_executive_summary`` from PI-074. This commit is UI-only.

exactly the named SHAs in chronological order, bypassing the
since..HEAD range entirely. Needed when multiple workstreams interleave

commit_message_full:
v2: PI-018 — add oneToOne YAML link-type support

Adds 'oneToOne' as a valid linkType in the YAML schema. YAML authors
can now declare a true 1:1 relationship and the engine creates it
in EspoCRM with the correct asymmetric internal types (hasOne on one
side, belongsTo on the inverse).

Today, methodology-side artifacts (interview-entity-prd.md, the Path B
importer) already accept oneToOne, but the YAML validator rejects it
— so requirements captured through the methodology can become
undeployable when they hit the engine. The first concrete trigger was
the CBM MN-Partner-TEST.yaml / MN-Sponsor-TEST.yaml scaffolding (2026-05-19),
worked around at the time with manyToOne + operator-discipline
uniqueness rules.

Changes:

- ``espo_impl/core/config_loader.py`` — ``VALID_LINK_TYPES`` grows
  ``oneToOne``. ``_validate_relationship`` rejects oneToOne missing
  ``linkForeign`` and warns on oneToOne with ``relationName`` (a
  manyToMany affordance).
- ``espo_impl/core/relationship_manager.py`` — ``LINK_TYPE_TO_METADATA``
  / ``_build_payload`` extended to handle the asymmetric oneToOne case.
  ``_links_match`` updated so the comparator compares oneToOne links
  correctly when reading back the per-side metadata type from EspoCRM.
- Unit tests cover: valid oneToOne accepted, missing-linkForeign
  rejected, relationName warns, asymmetric payload emitted, comparator
  matches oneToOne round-trip.
- ``PRDs/product/app-yaml-schema.md`` Section 6 documents oneToOne.

Out of scope (deferred): step 7 of the PI body (updating CBM repo
MN-Partner-TEST.yaml / MN-Sponsor-TEST.yaml to drop the manyToOne
workaround) — that's a separate-repo follow-on. End-to-end EspoCRM
deploy verification also deferred — no live EspoCRM available in this
autonomous run; unit + integration coverage carries the change.

commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-099 — fix apply_close_out section ordering so conversation POSTs before session
commit_message_full:
v2: PI-099 — fix apply_close_out section ordering so conversation POSTs before session

Every close-out's first apply attempt has been failing on the new
post-PI-073 ``complete_session_requires_conversation`` validation rule
since the redesign landed. Three back-to-back occurrences (SES-101
first apply at DEP-088, SES-103 first apply at DEP-091, SES-105 first
apply at DEP-094) traced to the same cause: ``_SECTIONS`` orders
``session`` before ``conversation``, but ``complete`` sessions require
at least one inbound ``conversation_belongs_to_session`` edge at
create-validation time. The conversation that emits that edge wasn't
POSTed yet.

The fix is a one-line list swap: process ``conversation`` before
``session``. Empirically proven safe — conversation creation already
tolerates a not-yet-existent ``conversation_belongs_to_session``
target (the three first-apply failures above ALL successfully created
the conversation; the session was the only thing that failed). With
the swap, the conversation's inline membership edge to the session is
in place before session validation runs, and the apply completes in
one pass.

Changes:

- ``crmbuilder-v2/scripts/apply_close_out.py`` — swap the first two
  entries of ``_SECTIONS``. Update the module docstring's apply-order
  diagram to match.
- ``tests/crmbuilder_v2/scripts/test_apply_close_out.py`` — assert
  the new section order, plus a single-pass-apply test that authors a
  minimal close-out payload and verifies the session lands in
  ``complete`` status without needing a retry.

The fix is independent of and complementary to PI-090 (apply-pipeline
validator). PI-090 will catch broader payload-shape violations at
pre-flight; PI-099 fixes the specific ordering bug that was tripping
every close-out.

From PI-099.

commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-090 — apply-pipeline validator for close-out payloads (post-PI-073)
commit_message_full:
v2: PI-090 — apply-pipeline validator for close-out payloads (post-PI-073)

Adds a close-out-payload validator that runs in apply_close_out.py
pre-flight, checking every payload against the governance recording
rules BEFORE any record is POSTed. Catches violations early with
actionable errors instead of partial applies that leave orphan records.

The PI body's check list predated PI-073; this implementation adapts
to the current session/conversation model:

- Decisions are decided_in a CONVERSATION (CNV-NNN), not a session.
- Conversation parentage is conversation_belongs_to_session, not the
  retired conversation_belongs_to_workstream / conversation_records_session.
- Sessions have no session_date field; that check is dropped.

Hard-reject checks (block the apply, exit non-zero before any POST):
required payload sections; session block shape + session_medium vocab
(would have caught SES-101's session_medium='claude_code'); conversation
block shape + conversation_belongs_to_session edge presence (makes the
complete_session_requires_conversation rule satisfiable); decision
status enum with explicit Final rejection (SES-067 class); decision
decided_in->conversation back-reference; PI item_type=pending_work;
work_ticket_file_path presence; reference field-key 'relationship' not
'relationship_kind' (SES-051/052 class); executive_summary 200-800
length (PI-074 rule, caught pre-flight).

Warning checks (print, do not block): identifier-head conflicts — a
warning rather than a reject because legitimate idempotent re-applies
reuse existing identifiers; true collisions are caught by the API 409.

Architecture: closeout_validator.py is a standalone module with a
validate_payload(payload, api_base=None) entry point. Shape checks run
offline; the head check needs the API. apply_close_out.py calls it
after the reachability check and before the section loop; a
--skip-validation flag bypasses it for emergencies.

Validated against the three most recent real payloads (ses_103,
ses_105, ses_107) — all pass with zero errors.

Enforcement layer #4 from the SES-096 strategy (DEC-310 layered
governance enforcement). Complements PI-099 (which fixed the specific
section-ordering bug) by catching the broader payload-shape class.

From PI-090.

commit_message_first_line: v2: MCP server runs its own Google-backed OAuth 2.1 + PKCE authorization server
commit_message_full:
v2: MCP server runs its own Google-backed OAuth 2.1 + PKCE authorization server

Replaces Cloudflare Managed OAuth, whose Beta dropped the per-request
resource and code_challenge params across its login step — empirically
confirmed (a code_challenge can never be a registration/config issue, so
its post-login disappearance proves the params are dropped), which blocked
claude.ai custom-connector registration and was misattributed to a claude.ai
bug in DEC-244.

The mcp SDK's OAuthAuthorizationServerProvider implementation preserves
code_challenge + resource by construction; human auth delegates to Google
OIDC (Internal app, dougbower.com). Access tokens are stateless HS256 JWTs;
clients + refresh tokens persist in a gitignored SQLite store. Verified
end-to-end locally: DCR, /authorize -> Google -> callback, PKCE token
exchange, JWT bearer validation, /mcp auth gate (401 without, tool call with).

commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-102 — reconcile executive_summary nullability across source layers
commit_message_full:
v2: PI-102 — reconcile executive_summary nullability across source layers

The live CRMBUILDER database has enforced NOT NULL + a 200-800 char CHECK
on executive_summary (decisions, planning_items) and
session_executive_summary (sessions) since migration 0023 (PI-075), but
the SQLAlchemy models still declared the columns nullable with a
permissive "IS NULL OR (length...)" CHECK. The model — and the access,
schema, MCP, and UI layers built on it — disagreed with the database it
describes. This reconciles all of them to the live schema. No new
migration: 0023 already formalized the constraint; this is the
source-of-record catch-up.

- models.py: three columns -> Mapped[str], nullable=False; CHECK drops
  the IS NULL arm. conversations.conversation_executive_summary is left
  nullable (migration 0023 deliberately excluded it; not yet backfilled).
- _helpers.py: add validate_required_length (rejects None/empty/short).
- decisions / planning_items / sessions repos: create paths now require
  executive_summary; update/patch require a valid value when the key is
  present (a NOT NULL column cannot be cleared). Turns an omitted summary
  into a clean 400/422 instead of a DB IntegrityError (500).
- api/schemas.py: create bodies + SessionReplaceIn (PUT) require the
  field; partial update/patch bodies stay optional.
- mcp_server/tools.py: create_decision / create_planning_item /
  create_session tools take a required executive_summary argument.
- UI schemas (decision / planning_item / session): Executive Summary
  field is required=True (was required=False + omit_when_empty_in_create),
  making the form honest about the NOT NULL constraint.
- tests: thread a valid summary through the affected create/POST/dialog
  sites; add test_executive_summary_required.py (access + api) locking in
  the NOT-NULL schema shape, access-layer rejection, the 422/400 API
  contract, and that conversations stays nullable.

Verified: full access+api and ui+mcp suites show zero new failures vs the
b13707c baseline; the 14 new contract tests pass.

Governance close-out (resolving PI-102 in the DB via close-out payload +
apply_close_out.py) is deferred: apply_close_out is currently broken on
this base for the PI-073 session shape.

v2: PI-078 — POST /identifiers/reserve endpoint

Lets the orchestrator atomically reserve a block of prefixed identifiers
(e.g. the next five SES) at the start of a run so concurrent child agents
never race on next-available numbers when posting close-out records.

- migration 0026 + IdentifierReservation model: one row per reserved
  block (entity_type, reserved_identifiers, max_number, reserved_by,
  reserved_at, expires_at). Ephemeral runtime state — deliberately not in
  the snapshot exporter.

  max(table_max, active_reservation_max) + 1, reusing each entity type's
  canonical next-identifier function and parsing prefix/width from it

  row, table_max covers it (implicit consume); expired blocks are purged
  and ignored (TTL auto-release, default 1h). Reservable: session,
  decision, planning_item, risk, topic, conversation (charter/status are
  version singletons, references are numeric — excluded).
